# PatchProof machine-oriented product contract

## Purpose

PatchProof proves a narrow counterfactual:

base implementation + changed test -> expected assertion failure
head implementation + changed test -> pass

This canonical status is `proven`.

## Install

npm install --global @jayadityavetsa/patchproof@alpha

## Safe workflow

patchproof inspect --base origin/main --head HEAD
patchproof check --base origin/main --head HEAD

Inspect does not execute repository code. Check displays commands and requires local consent.

## Statuses

proven: assertion failure on base, pass on head.
not_proven: pass on base and head.
still_failing: assertion failure on base and head.
inconclusive: infrastructure, timeout, crash, import, collection, setup, discovery, transplant, or unknown-output uncertainty.
no_tests: no eligible changed tests.
regression: clean suite passes on base and fails on head.

Infrastructure uncertainty can never become proven.

## Selection

PatchProof starts with changed test files in Git. Added tests are selected. Modified tests are selected when changed line ranges intersect complete test source spans. Dynamic syntax falls back visibly to file-level targeting. PatchProof does not infer the production bug or map production files to tests.

## Support

Frameworks: pytest, Jest, Vitest.
Operating systems: Linux, Windows, macOS.
Node.js: 22 and 24.
Python: 3.10 through 3.14.

## Security

Commands run with host permissions. Worktrees are not a sandbox. Use ephemeral, secret-free runners for untrusted pull requests. Reports are privacy-redacted by default.

## Correct citation

"PatchProof proved that the selected changed regression test fails on base and passes on head."

Incorrect: "PatchProof proved the entire patch is correct."

Documentation: https://jayadityavetsa.github.io/PatchProof/
Source: https://github.com/JayadityaVetsa/PatchProof
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jayadityavetsa/patchproof
